description: The 2004 Australian federal election became a referendum on the protection of Tasmania's old-growth forests. PM John Howard campaigned on supporting the timber industry, while the Greens campaigned for total protection, resulting in Howard's victory.

key_events.description: The Liberal Party announces a policy to allow continued logging to secure rural worker votes.
Environmental groups launch a national 'Save the Forests' campaign targeting urban voters.
Election day exit polls show economic anxiety outweighs ecological conservation concerns for most voters.

calibration_notes: Illustrates how economic security narratives can override environmental protection during national elections.
